economic_notes: 5 band-owned subsidiaries accounted for under modified equity method: Bamaji Lake Economic Development Corporation, Knobby's Fly-In Camps Limited, Bamaji Air Inc. (75% owned), 2159256 Ontario Ltd., 1902070 Ontario Inc. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership income of $930,450. Hydro One agreement provides $63,912/year for 25 years for land occupation on reserve. Settlement trust from Lake St. Joseph flood claim with $5,400,000 original compensation.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2020 but audit report signed February 11, 2022 — significant delay. Five subsidiaries accounted for under modified equity method (GBEs), not fully consolidated. Fully consolidated entities include CMHC, Land Use Planning, Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership, and Slate Falls Nation Trust. Current liabilities calculated as accounts payable $760,009 + wages payable $167,667 + employee deductions payable $143,055 = $1,070,731; deferred revenue includes deferred income $19,725 + incomplete projects $2,042,919 = $2,062,644. ISC Band Representative funding of $57,654 recognized in current year though approved for 2020/21. No schedule of remuneration included. Prior accumulated surplus is opening balance of $22,745,634 per statement. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $3,058,559 + Health Canada $3,469,782 = $6,528,341; OSR = $11,621,377 - $6,528,341 = $5,093,036 (includes subsidiary income, ONFLP, interest, other program funding).
